---
title: "Week 1 - Getting Started"
toc: true
toc-title: "On this page"
---

## Course Introduction {#course-introduction}

::: {.video-placeholder}
🎬 **Welcome Video**

*Your welcome video will go here. To embed a YouTube video, replace this block with:*

```{{=html}}
<iframe width="100%" height="415" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/YOUR-VIDEO-ID" 
title="Course Introduction" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>
```

:::
---

### Getting Set Up

Before diving into the content, complete these three steps to get your course environment ready.

::: {.setup-cards}

::: {.setup-card}
### 1. Join Google Classroom
Click the link below to join the course Google Classroom. This is where you'll submit assignments, participate in discussions, and receive feedback.

[→ Join Google Classroom](https://classroom.google.com){.btn .btn-primary target="_blank"}
:::

::: {.setup-card}
### 2. Bookmark this site
Add this course site to your browser bookmarks - it's your home base for all course content, lecture videos, and module instructions. Every week you'll start here.
:::

::: {.setup-card}
### 3. Make a copy of the Portfolio Template
Your **Pacific CI Portfolio** is a Google Doc you'll add to each week. By Week 5 it becomes the foundation of your final project.

[→ Make a copy of the Portfolio Template](https://docs.google.com/){.btn .btn-primary target="_blank"}

*Once you've made your copy, keep the link handy - you'll submit it in Google Classroom each week.*
:::

:::

---

### Course Outcomes

By the end of this course you will be able to:

::: {.outcomes-grid}

::: {.outcome-card}
**🌏 Understand CI in context**
Define cyberinfrastructure and explain why it matters specifically for Small Island Developing States and Pacific sustainability challenges.
:::

::: {.outcome-card}
**🛠️ Navigate real tools**
Identify and explore key CI tools and platforms - including NSF resources, science gateways, and cloud platforms - relevant to Pacific sustainability research.
:::

::: {.outcome-card}
**📊 Bridge data and decisions**
Analyze how CI outputs are translated into actionable indicators and policy decisions, and recognize where gaps and equity concerns arise.
:::

::: {.outcome-card}
**📄 Produce a policy brief**
Design a CI-informed policy brief proposing how cyberinfrastructure could improve sustainability decision-making for a Pacific island nation of your choosing.
:::

:::

---

### How This Course Works

Each week follows the same rhythm - so once you know the pattern, you always know what to expect.

::: {.flow-steps}

::: {.flow-step}
::: {.flow-step-number}
1
:::
::: {.flow-step-content}
**Watch the lecture video**
Each module has a short (~10 min) lecture video embedded on the week's page. Watch it first before moving on.
:::
:::

::: {.flow-step}
::: {.flow-step-number}
2
:::
::: {.flow-step-content}
**Read through the module**
Read through the week's content that are reflected in the lecture video.
:::
:::

::: {.flow-step}
::: {.flow-step-number}
3
:::
::: {.flow-step-content}
**Complete the mini-assignment**
As described in the mini-assignment for each week, add a new section to your Pacific CI Portfolio and submit the link in Google Classroom by Sunday 11:59pm HST.
:::
:::

::: {.flow-step}
::: {.flow-step-number}
4
:::
::: {.flow-step-content}
**Join the discussion**
Respond to the weekly discussion prompt in Google Classroom - 2–3 sentences minimum.
:::
:::

:::

---

### Course Project

Over the four instructional weeks you'll build a **Pacific CI Portfolio** - a living document that captures your work week by week. In Weeks 5 and 6 you'll assemble that portfolio into a **policy brief** proposing how cyberinfrastructure could improve sustainability decision-making for a Pacific island nation of your choosing.

No prior technical experience is needed. The policy brief is assessed on your reasoning, your use of real CI tools and datasets from the course, and your ability to identify gaps and equity concerns honestly.

[→ Read more about the Final Project](project.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

---

### ✅ Tasks to Complete - Module 1A

Complete these before moving on to Module 1B.

::: {.task-list}

::: {.task-item}
☐ **Join Google Classroom** - use the link in the Getting Set Up section above
:::

::: {.task-item}
☐ **Make a copy of the Portfolio Template** - keep the link somewhere important
:::

::: {.task-item}
☐ **Share your portfolio link** - submit it in the Week 1 Google Classroom assignment (make sure you set the correct sharing permissions so I can see it!)
:::

::: {.task-item}
☐ **Introduce yourself** - post a short intro in the Week 1 discussion thread in Google Classroom (name, where you're from, why you joined the course, etc.)
:::

:::

---

::: {.next-module}
Ready? Let's get started! →  [Module 1B: CI Foundations](week1.qmd#ci-foundations)
:::

---

## CI Foundations {#ci-foundations}

::: {.module-tag}
Module 1B
:::

### 🪝 Hook

Pacific island communities are already leading in sustainability and climate action - combining traditional knowledge, local expertise, and scientific tools to address some of the world's most urgent sustainability challenges. But accessing the data and computational tools that could support that work isn't always straightforward.

This module introduces **cyberinfrastructure** - a powerful set of tools that can help bridge the gap between the data that exists and the decisions that need to be made.

> *"Pacific Islanders are leading climate action and combining their own systems of knowledge with western science to implement locally relevant climate solutions."*
> — Frontiers in Marine Science, 2019

::: {.resource-link}
📖 **Recommended reading:** [Lessons From the Pacific Islands – Adapting to Climate Change by Supporting Social and Ecological Resilience](https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/marine-science/articles/10.3389/fmars.2019.00289/full){target="_blank"} — read the introduction (~2 pages). Open access.
:::

---

### 🎬 Lecture Video

::: {.video-placeholder}
**CI Foundations - Lecture Video (~10 min)**

*Your lecture video will go here. To embed a YouTube video, replace this block with:*

```{{=html}}
<iframe width="100%" height="415" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/YOUR-VIDEO-ID" 
title="CI Foundations" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>
```

:::

---

### Hands-On Use Case - Pacific Data Hub

The **Pacific Data Hub** is a regional cyberinfrastructure platform aggregating climate, fisheries, ocean, and demographic data from 25 Pacific nations. This is cyberinfrastructure in action - a shared regional platform that gives researchers and decision-makers across the Pacific access to data they couldn't build or maintain independently.

::: {.fieldtrip-box}
**Your task this week:**

Visit [pacificdata.org](https://pacificdata.org){target="_blank"} and spend 10-20 minutes exploring before completing the mini assignment below.

**What to look for:**

- What kinds of data are available for your chosen island?
- Who collected it and when was it last updated?
- What sustainability challenges does it connect to?
- What feels missing?
:::

---

### 📋 Mini Assignment - Data Audit

::: {.assignment-box}
**Week 1 Assignment - Data Audit**

Using the Pacific Data Hub, choose **one Pacific island country** - this will be your focus for all four portfolio entries throughout the course. Find **two datasets** tied to a sustainability challenge (sea level, freshwater, fisheries, coral reefs, food security, or another challenge relevant to your island).

**For each dataset, answer these four prompts:**

1. **Decision value:** What specific question could a local planner, official, or community leader answer using this data?
2. **Who decides:** Name one real role who would use this — e.g. fisheries officer, disaster risk manager, mayor.
3. **Data gap:** What is missing, outdated, or too coarse to make that decision confidently?
4. **Infrastructure gap:** Is the gap a data problem, a connectivity problem, a capacity problem - or all three?

Add your responses to the **Week 1 section of your Pacific CI Portfolio** Google Doc. When you're done, submit your portfolio link in Google Classroom.

[→ Submit Week 1 Assignment in Google Classroom](https://classroom.google.com){.btn .btn-primary target="_blank"}
:::

---

### Discussion Prompt

**This week's discussion in Google Classroom.**

> *"Before we dive in - what do you think of when you hear the word 'cyberinfrastructure'? No wrong answers. Just your honest first impression."*

Respond in 2–3 sentences minimum.

[→ Go to Week 1 Discussion in Google Classroom](https://classroom.google.com){.btn .btn-outline-primary target="_blank"}

---

### Closing Thoughts

> *"The Pacific Data Hub now gives a fisheries officer in Nauru access to regional ocean data that would have taken weeks to gather a decade ago. That's what shared cyberinfrastructure makes possible - and you're just getting started."*

---

### ✅ Tasks to Complete - Module 1B

::: {.task-list}

::: {.task-item}
☐ **Watch the lecture video** - CI Foundations (~10 min)
:::

::: {.task-item}
☐ **Go through the hands-on use case** - explore the Pacific Data Hub at pacificdata.org
:::

::: {.task-item}
☐ **Submit your mini assignment** - add the data audit to your Pacific CI Portfolio and submit the link in Google Classroom
:::

::: {.task-item}
☐ **Submit the discussion** - respond to the Week 1 discussion prompt in Google Classroom
:::

:::

---

::: {.next-module}
Up next → [Week 2: The Pacific Context and CI Tools](week2.qmd)
:::
